title: JAMB EXTENDS 2026 DIRECT ENTRY REGISTRATION TO MAY 8
author: BIG QUAMS MEDIA
summary: JAMB has extended the 2026 Direct Entry registration deadline to May 8, 2026. This gives eligible candidates who haven’t applied a fresh chance to complete registration.
fullContent:
JAMB has extended the 2026 Direct Entry registration deadline to May 8, 2026. This gives eligible candidates who haven’t applied a fresh chance to complete registration.

The Board had earlier fixed April 25, 2026 as the closing date after starting DE registration in March. The new extension was approved to accommodate more applicants.

Students using DE should use this window wisely. Get your E-PIN, visit an accredited centre, and avoid last-minute rush. Don’t assume there will be another extension.

title: JAMB FIXES 2026/2027 ADMISSIONS POLICY MEETING FOR MAY 11
author: BIG QUAMS MEDIA
summary: JAMB has announced the 2026/2027 Admissions Policy Meeting for all tertiary institutions. The meeting is scheduled for May 11th, 2026 and will set guidelines for the new admission cycle.
fullContent:
JAMB has announced the 2026/2027 Admissions Policy Meeting for all tertiary institutions. The meeting is scheduled for May 11th, 2026 and will set guidelines for the new admission cycle.

The policy meeting will determine cut-off marks, admission quotas, and timelines for universities, polytechnics, and colleges. All institutions must attend to align with JAMB directives for 2026/2027 admissions.

Students awaiting admission should note this date. After the meeting, schools will release Post-UTME forms and official cut-off marks. Stay updated and avoid relying on fake admission news.

title: JAMB CRACKS DOWN: 23 CBT CENTRES DELISTED, 88 UNDER WATCH AHEAD OF 2026 UTME
author: BIG QUAMS MEDIA
summary:
JAMB has removed 23 CBT centres over major technical failures during the mock UTME exercise.
An additional 88 centres have been warned to urgently fix minor issues or risk sanctions.
fullContent:
The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has taken decisive action ahead of the 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME), delisting 23 Computer-Based Test (CBT) centres across the country.

This development follows a comprehensive assessment conducted during the recently held mock UTME, where the affected centres were found to have significant technical deficiencies that compromised their ability to deliver a seamless examination experience.

In addition to the delisted centres, JAMB revealed that 88 other CBT centres have been placed on notice over minor technical shortcomings. These centres have been given strict directives to address the identified issues promptly or face possible delisting before the main examination begins.

JAMB emphasized that the move is part of its broader commitment to maintaining the integrity, credibility, and smooth conduct of its examinations nationwide. The board reassured candidates and stakeholders that only fully compliant and technically sound centres will be accredited for the 2026 UTME.

The board further advised candidates to stay informed and ensure they print their examination slips early to confirm their assigned centres and schedules.

title: WAEC Unveils 2026 WASSCE Timetable: Exam Begins April 7
author: BIG QUAMS MEDIA
summary: The West African Examinations Council (WAEC) has officially released the final timetable for the 2026 West African Senior School Certificate Examination (WASSCE). The exams will commence on April 7, 2026, starting with practical and project-based papers and concluding in mid-June 2026.
fullContent:
In an official release, the West African Examinations Council (WAEC) has unveiled the 2026 WASSCE timetable for school candidates. According to the timetable, the exams will officially begin on Tuesday, April 7, 2026, with project-based and practical papers. The examinations will then proceed through a structured sequence of oral, practical, and written papers.

This multi-week examination process will continue until mid-June 2026, marking the conclusion of the academic journey for candidates across WAEC member countries.

Candidates are encouraged to prepare in line with the schedule to ensure a smooth examination experience. The full timetable has been made available through WAEC’s headquarters in Accra and is accessible for all stakeholders.

title: JAMB Delists 20 CBT Centres, Mock UTME Results Released
author: BIG QUAMS MEDIA
summary: JAMB has officially released the results of the 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME) Mock held on March 28. Additionally, over 20 CBT centres have been delisted due to technical issues. The main UTME is set to begin on April 16, 2026.
fullContent:
In a recent update, the Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has announced the release of the 2026 UTME Mock results, which were held on Saturday, March 28, 2026. A total of 224,597 candidates registered for the mock examination, with 152,586 candidates successfully taking the test across 989 Computer-Based Test (CBT) centres nationwide.

However, following the mock examination, JAMB has delisted over 20 CBT centres due to technical challenges and inadequate infrastructure that hindered the smooth conduct of the test. The affected centres have been removed from the list of approved examination venues for the upcoming 2026 UTME.

Candidates who participated in the mock exam can check their results by sending MOCKRESULT via SMS to 55019 or 66019, using the phone number they registered with.

In a related warning, JAMB has cautioned candidates against fraudsters, particularly those operating on platforms like WhatsApp, who claim they can facilitate score inflation. JAMB made it clear that such claims are fraudulent and any candidate caught in such activities will have their registration canceled, with the possibility of having their results withheld or withdrawn if discovered after the examination.

Candidates are reminded that the main UTME will commence on Thursday, April 16, 2026, and all necessary preparations should be completed in advance.

title: NECO Sticks to 2026 SSCE Registration Deadlines—No Extension, Schools Warned
author: BIG QUAMS MEDIA
summary: According to a recent report, the National Examinations Council (NECO) has made it clear that there will be no extension for the 2026 Senior Secondary Certificate Examination (SSCE) registration. Schools are urged to adhere strictly to the registration deadlines to avoid any disruptions for candidates.
fullContent:
According to the latest statement from the National Examinations Council (NECO), there will be no extensions granted for the registration of candidates for the 2026 Senior Secondary Certificate Examination (SSCE). Schools have been reminded that the deadline for normal registration is Monday, 25th May 2026, while late registration will end on Friday, 29th May 2026.

The council emphasized that schools must adhere strictly to these dates to ensure that all candidates are appropriately registered. This move comes as part of NECO's effort to streamline the examination process, ensuring a smooth and organized cycle for the upcoming SSCE.

NECO's registrar has also highlighted that meeting these deadlines is essential for the proper administration of the exam. Failure to register within the specified periods could lead to students being excluded from sitting for the SSCE, potentially affecting their future academic opportunities, including university admissions and scholarship applications.

The warning stresses the importance of preparing early, as no further extensions will be granted, signaling NECO's commitment to maintaining a strict and efficient schedule for the 2026 examination cycle.
